  "findings": [
    "Hydrogen Sulfide (H2S) level is 40 ppm, which exceeds the acceptable entry limit of 10 ppm and is immediately dangerous to life or health (IDLH) at 100 ppm."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Deny entry to the confined space.",
    "Ventilate the space thoroughly and re-test the atmosphere before allowing any entry.",
    "Ensure workers do not enter without appropriate supplied-air respirators if entry is absolutely necessary before ventilation."
  ],
  "rationale": "The 4-gas monitor shows an H2S reading of 40 ppm. Under OSHA 1910.146, the acceptable entry limit for H2S is typically 10 ppm (or the PEL of 10 ppm as an 8-hour TWA). A reading of 40 ppm is highly toxic and unsafe for standard permit-required confined space entry without specialized controls and respiratory protection.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.146",
    "OSHA 1910.1000 Table Z-2"
  ]
}
